key_insight: Parallel-route coherent mixup (PaRC-mix) improves prostate lesion segmentation generalization across MRI scanner domains by augmenting features at multiple network layers.
clinical_relevance: Better cross-site segmentation could support more reliable MRI-guided adaptive radiotherapy for prostate cancer, where accurate daily delineation is essential.
method: Trained two segmentation models: MRRN and UNet++.
Applied PaRC-mix to create linear feature combinations at multiple network layers using samples from the same batch.
Compared against no-mixup and input-mixup approaches for dominant intraprostatic lesion segmentation on ADC MRI.
evidence_signal: Models trained on 2,029 samples from 3.0T GE MRI were tested on 1,547 samples from five external datasets spanning 3T Siemens, 3T Philips, and 1.5T Elekta Unity scanners, with PaRC-mix improving detection, segmentation, and composite accuracy metrics.
watch_item: Results are promising but remain specific to one prostate lesion task and MRI domain setup, so broader clinical adoption would need validation across additional centers, protocols, and targets.
paper_title: Enhancing Prostate Cancer Segmentation for Multi-Domain Generalization using a novel Parallel-Route Coherent Mixup Regularization Training
